Inside The McDonnell Douglas MD-11's Flight Deck

The McDonnell Douglas MD-11's cockpit represented a pivotal moment in aviation history where analog transitioned into digital.

The MD-11 cockpit demonstrated how the aviation industry successfully integrated legacy analog systems with emerging digital avionics during a critical technological transition period.

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their Safety Management System compliance to the FAA, establishing a verifiable audit trail that demonstrates systematic hazard identification and risk mitigation.

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Alaska Airlines' 787-9 deployment signals its competitive pivot toward premium international markets, leveraging existing seat inventory to enter long-haul markets typically dominated by legacy carriers.

From Boeing's first commercial aircraft launched in 1933 to the modern 787 and 777X families, these aircraft were instrumental in shaping aviation.

Boeing's evolutionary aircraft designs from the 1933 Model 247 through today's widebodies established the technological and operational standards that competitors still benchmark against.

As airlines compete for seamless digital experiences, Delta's partnership with Amazon's satellite network marks a significant leap in onboard Wi-Fi.

Delta's adoption of satellite-based connectivity bypasses ground infrastructure limitations, enabling consistent high-speed internet across remote routes where traditional systems fail.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

How Airbus Aircraft Noses Differ To Boeing's

Why do aircraft made by Boeing feature slightly different noses to those made by Airbus?

Airbus nose designs prioritize crew visibility and aerodynamic efficiency, while Boeing emphasizes cargo-door accessibility and structural integration, reflecting divergent engineering philosophies in fuselage design.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 5 Airlines With The Largest Narrowbody Fleets In The United States

American, United, Delta, Southwest, and Alaska lead the US narrowbody fleet counts with collectively over 3,500 single-aisle aircraft.

The dominance of five carriers in narrowbody aircraft represents consolidated market power that shapes route frequency, aircraft utilization strategies, and maintenance network development across North America.

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as critical infrastructure for flight training pipelines while maintaining capacity for commercial carriers, addressing the aviation industry's ongoing pilot shortage challenge.

Switlik: Built for Survival

For more than a century, Switlik Survival Products has engineered customizable aviation survival solutions trusted by pilots and first responders worldwide.

Switlik's century-long specialization in customizable survival equipment positions it as irreplaceable infrastructure for aviation safety across commercial, military, and emergency response sectors.

Textron's single-engine Skyhawk continues competing for training and personal-use missions alongside more expensive twin-engine business jets in shared hangar environments.

Military Mar 31, 2026

Pentagon Considers Anti-Drone Lasers in Washington

Reported drone activity near Fort McNair has prompted review of counter-UAS system.

Increasing unauthorized drone incursions near military installations are pushing the Pentagon toward directed-energy weapons, signaling a shift from traditional intercept methods to permanent airspace protection infrastructure.

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight planning offers immediate capacity and efficiency gains without waiting for new aircraft technology deployment.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Gone After Just 1 Year: Virgin Atlantic Axes This Long-Haul Route From London

Virgin Atlantic’s Riyadh exit looks abrupt, but the bigger story is where the aircraft goes next.

Aircraft redeployment from failed routes reveals capacity constraints forcing carriers to choose between underperforming long-haul markets and higher-yield regional expansions.

Fighter jet hourly operating costs exceed $30,000-$70,000 depending on aircraft type, significantly impacting military budget allocation and acquisition decisions.

ISS research directly validates human spaceflight systems and procedures essential for lunar return missions, reducing technical risk.

As airlines across the globe grapple with a serious jet fuel price crisis that has sent costs soaring…...

Trump's energy nationalism policy may fragment global fuel supply chains, forcing airlines to secure regional supplies at premium costs rather than relying on integrated international markets.

EASA: Narrow Mideast Corridors Increase Operational Risk

European regulators say conflict-related airspace restrictions are increasing congestion and risks.

Compressed routing through Middle East conflict zones is forcing higher-density traffic concentration, amplifying collision risks and reducing pilot response time margins.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry

In two recent studies, researchers used Landsat data to fill key gaps in our knowledge of reservoir structure and dynamics.

Landsat's earth observation capabilities enable cost-effective monitoring of reservoir bathymetry and water level changes, with applications for sustainable water resource management in aviation-dependent regions.

New system will provide higher-resolution weather data for aviation users.
